seoTitle: How to Use LocalWP to Build WordPress Sites Offline
metaDescription: Learn how to install LocalWP, create a local WordPress site, and add themes and plugins to build and test websites offline before hosting them.
intro: LocalWP lets you spin up a full WordPress website on your own computer with a single click, no hosting or domain required. This guide covers installing LocalWP, creating a local WordPress site, and setting up themes, plugins, and Elementor for testing. It's aimed at beginners who want a safe place to practice WordPress or test plugins before going live.

steps.heading: Download and install LocalWP
steps.body: Get LocalWP for Windows or Mac and complete the installation before creating your first site.

steps.heading: Create a WordPress site locally
steps.body: Use LocalWP's site creation wizard to spin up a working WordPress installation on your computer in seconds.

steps.heading: Install themes and plugins
steps.body: Log in to your local WordPress dashboard and install themes, plugins, or page builders like Elementor just as you would on a live site.

steps.heading: Use database and SSL tools
steps.body: Access LocalWP's built-in database tools and enable SSL to test your site under conditions closer to a real hosting environment.

steps.heading: Fix common issues and export for hosting
steps.body: Troubleshoot problems like the site not loading, then export your finished local site when you're ready to move it to real hosting.

faq.question: Is LocalWP free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, LocalWP is free and designed to let anyone build and test WordPress sites on their own computer without hosting costs.

faq.question: Can I move a site built in LocalWP to a live host later?
faq.answer: Yes, LocalWP includes an export feature specifically for pushing your locally built site to a hosting provider when it's ready.

faq.question: Why isn't my LocalWP site loading?
faq.answer: This is often related to a local server conflict or a site that hasn't fully started, so restarting the site or checking LocalWP's logs usually resolves it.

faq.question: Does LocalWP work with Elementor and other page builders?
faq.answer: Yes, you can install Elementor and other plugins on a LocalWP site the same way you would on any WordPress installation.
